Determination of energy dependence of NU for
uranium-238, plutonium-240 and plutonium-241 from an
analysis of the fission energy balance .
The method of measuring mass and kinetic energy
distributions of the fragments has been described
Three rel.refs for analysis on the basis of balance
including the results of measurements on mass and
kinetic energy distributions of fragments during
Simultaneous measurement of light and heavy fragments

NU was derived by fission energy balance (method
described in rel.ref. of Kolosov+) , data from rel.ref.

NU was derived by fission energy balance (method
described in rel.ref. of Kolosov+) , data from rel.ref.

The energy dependence of NU(En) was expressed as:
NUthermal - average number of prompt neutrons at Pu-241
dEk - the change in fragment kinetic energy not
associated with a change in fragment yields;
dNUj - the change in the number of prompt neutrons
resulting only from difference in fragment yields for
neutrons of energy En and for thermal neurons.
dEk and dNUj were determined from exp. measurement of
ALPHA - coefficient determined using the exp.data of
this work and data of other authors; it was assumed
that ALPHA increases linearly from 0.094 1/MeV for
thermal neutron fission to 0.120 1/MeV fro 5 MeV
measurements (+-100 keV) and error of ALPHA coefficient
"PART-DET (FF)" was moved from Subent 001 according to
